name: Mantling
description: A traversal action where the player climbs or vaults onto a ledge after detecting a reachable surface.
design_notes: Mantling is a contract between collision probes, animation, and player intent. It must identify a valid ledge, reserve a safe target position, and restore control cleanly. If probes are too permissive, players mantle through geometry; if too strict, obvious ledges feel broken.
design_purpose: Let players recover onto ledges or low obstacles without requiring a full jump arc.
Support parkour routes, cover traversal, and forgiving vertical movement.
Make level geometry feel tactile and climbable where designers intend it.
player_fantasy: The player feels capable of catching and climbing over reachable edges with physical intent.

parameters.name: mantle_probe_height
parameters.description: Vertical search band for detecting reachable ledges.

parameters.name: mantle_forward_probe_distance
parameters.description: Forward distance used to find ledge fronts.

parameters.name: mantle_target_clearance_height
parameters.description: Clear space required at the final standing position.

parameters.name: mantle_duration_ms
parameters.description: Time spent in the mantle animation or movement warp.

parameters.name: mantle_cancel_after_ms
parameters.description: Earliest point where damage, jump, or drop can cancel the action.

edge_cases: Moving ledges, elevators, and vehicles need target positions relative to the moving surface.
Crouch-height spaces above the ledge should choose crouched finish, reject, or crawl transition.
Mantle probes should reject thin railings, decorative trim, hazards, and one-way collision unless tagged.
Multiplayer mantling needs reservation or overlap handling at the landing position.
common_bugs: The final target point is valid during detection but occupied by another actor before the animation finishes.
Root motion moves the character through walls because collision is disabled for the whole animation.
Mantle state is interrupted by damage and never restores input, gravity, or collision masks.
Probe order picks a high ledge behind a low ledge and causes a teleport-like climb.
balancing_notes: Fast mantles support flow and parkour; slow mantles create commitment in combat or stealth spaces.
Generous mantle detection can make routes forgiving but may invalidate jump skill and level gates.
accessibility_notes: Use clear ledge affordances and input buffering so players do not need exact jump-and-grab timing.
Avoid camera snaps during mantle animation, or provide reduced camera motion during automated traversal.

implementation_notes.unity: Use staged probes for wall, top surface, and final clearance, reserve the target point, then move with root motion or scripted interpolation while still checking collision.
implementation_notes.godot: Run ray or shape casts for ledge front and top clearance, store the mantle target relative to moving bodies, and blend movement in _physics_process.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use motion warping or root motion with ledge traces, target reservation, and a custom movement state that restores collision and input on every exit path.
implementation_notes.web: Perform ledge detection with collision queries, store target_position and target_surface_id, and interpolate through a mantle state while validating final clearance.

example_games.title: Uncharted 4: A Thief's End
example_games.note: Uses ledge mantling as a core traversal action.

example_games.title: Assassin's Creed II
example_games.note: Uses climb and mantle interactions across authored architecture.
